verdicts.title: apply-branch-protection.yml can never run — top-level return in inline Python
verdicts.note: apply-branch-protection.yml:54,60,74,84 use bare `return` at module scope inside `python3 << 'PY'` heredoc, a compile-time SyntaxError; branch-protection.json:50 has bypass_actors: [].

verdicts.title: CI runs no tests for frontend / chat-surface / chat-transport / surface-renderers
verdicts.note: ci-frontend.yml:47-52 only typechecks+builds; the sole `npm run test` in any workflow is ci-desktop.yml:75, while 157 frontend + 186 chat-surface test files with `test` scripts (apps/frontend/package.json:10, chat-surface:11) never run.

verdicts.title: CI/release path filters drifted from the real dependency graph
verdicts.note: ci-frontend.yml:5-8 filter excludes chat-surface (imported 169x) and chat-transport (3x); release-images.yml:12-24 omits packages/audit-chain though backend/ai-backend Dockerfiles pip-install it (Dockerfile:12-16) — sub-claim (c) surface-renderers is imprecise (0 frontend imports; it is in the desktop chain, not frontend).

verdicts.title: Production staged-rollout gate always fails for non-canary tiers
verdicts.note: deploy.yml:146 hard-codes synthesized prior records as {tier:"unknown",environment:"unknown"}, but check_staged_rollout.py:65-66 counts only tier==upstream(canary/early) AND environment=="production", so early/general exit 1 unless --force (force_deploy=true) is passed.

verdicts.title: Supply-chain rigor asymmetric across the three services
verdicts.note: ai-backend has no requirements.in and installs plain `pip install -r requirements.txt` in CI (ci-ai-backend.yml:47) and Dockerfile:18 (no --require-hashes), while backend/facade use requirements.in + --require-hashes (backend/Dockerfile:17, facade/Dockerfile:15) + pip-compile reproducibility check (ci-backend.yml:48-54).

verdicts.title: Python version skew: images 3.14, CI/venv 3.13, pyright 3.11
verdicts.note: All three service images are `FROM python:3.14-slim-bookworm` (backend/facade/ai-backend Dockerfile:1) while ci-backend.yml:37 pins 3.13, desktop-runtime/manifest.json:8 is 3.13.14, and pyrightconfig.json:11 declares 3.11.

verdicts.title: Migration-manifest logic triplicated; documented CI gate does not exist
verdicts.note: check_migration_manifest.py's parse/digest/render is byte-near-identical in backend/db/migrate.py:99-160 and ai-backend schema/migrate.py:95-158 (same sha256(up)+NUL+rollback algo, same `line.split(" sha256=")` parse, same auto-generated header saying 'CI will refuse'), yet grep of .github/Makefile/.pre-commit finds zero invocations; docs claim a CI gate (roadmap/02-c2:92,147; decisions/0002:39).

verdicts.title: branch-protection.json would deadlock merges if applied
verdicts.note: branch-protection.json:38-46 sets strict_required_status_checks_policy:true with contexts naming path-filtered workflows (ci-backend/frontend/etc.) that never report on unrelated PRs, and all three backend workflows share job name `test-and-audit` (ci-backend:30, ci-backend-facade:28, ci-ai-backend:30); bypass_actors: [] (line 50) leaves no escape.

verdicts.title: DEV_AUTH_BYPASS: docs say removed but build config still sets/honors it
verdicts.note: Low-severity, accepted as-is per verifier scope.

verdicts.title: nginx gateway config duplicated and already drifted (SSE read timeout)
verdicts.note: Low-severity, accepted as-is per verifier scope.

verdicts.title: deploy-website.yml uses floating action tags while others SHA-pin
verdicts.note: Low-severity, accepted as-is per verifier scope.

verdicts.title: policy-lint test suites have no runner
verdicts.note: Low-severity, accepted as-is per verifier scope.

verdicts.title: apps/website has no PR CI
verdicts.note: Low-severity, accepted as-is per verifier scope.

verdicts.title: Legacy env-var names in dev configs (MCP_TOKEN_VAULT_PROVIDER)
verdicts.note: Low-severity, accepted as-is per verifier scope.
